page.content: In the Claims loss ratio trends / Underwriting profit margins table for MetLife as of December 2023, the Claim Category header includes Property, Liability, Auto, Health, Travel, Commercial, and Reinsurance, reflecting Total Claims of £1,200,000, £800,000, £1,000,000, £1,500,000, £300,000, £900,000, and £2,500,000 respectively. Paid Claims are £800,000, £500,000, £700,000, £1,200,000, £250,000, £600,000, and £1,800,000, while Outstanding Claims are £400,000, £300,000, £300,000, £300,000, £50,000, £300,000, and £700,000. The Loss Ratio for these categories is 66.67%, 62.50%, 70.00%, 80.00%, 83.33%, 66.67%, and 72.00%, respectively, with all claims marked as Settled.

page.content: The overall Total Claims amount to £8,500,000, with Paid Claims at £5,850,000 and Outstanding Claims at £2,650,000, resulting in an overall Loss Ratio of 68.82%.

page.content: In the Financial Ratios and Profitability Metrics table, P/E Ratio is 12.5, Current Ratio is 1.2, Debt/Equity is 0.35, Return on Equity is 15%, Operating Metrics show an Underwriting Margin of 90%, Combined Ratio of 22%, and Expense Ratio of 5M GBP. Other metrics include Net Written Premiums at 200M GBP, Net Income at 300M GBP, Gross Premiums at 50M GBP, Investment Income at 1.8B GBP, Total Assets of 1.5B GBP, Total Liabilities at 1B GBP, with a Solvency Ratio of 1.3B GBP, Retention Ratio of 600M GBP, Claims Paid at 0.4, Claims Reserve at 95%, and Total Revenue at 30%.
